meta.name: russle
meta.tagline: Web design, development, and branding studio
meta.description: russle is a one-person studio designing, building, and branding modern websites for ambitious teams.

Navbar.behavior: transparent at top, on scroll past 80px adds bg-1 fill + 1px line2 bottom border, fixed top
Navbar.structure: logo (left) | nav links (center, hidden < md) | CTA (right)
Navbar.mobile: hamburger opens full-screen overlay with stacked links + CTA

Footer.bottomBar: © 2026 russle · built in this repo · privacy
Footer.decorative: huge 'russle' wordmark spanning the bottom edge in --color-dark2 (subtle)

variants.primary: fill: text, color: bg, hover bg: dark2
variants.accent: fill: accent, color: onAccent, hover bg: accentLo
variants.secondary: border: line2, color: text, hover bg: surface
variants.ghost: no border, color: text, hover underline + accent

Card.default: surface fill on bg, radius l (24px), padding 32-48px, no shadow
Card.onDark: dark2 fill on dark, radius l, onDark text

props: spacing: xs | s | m | l | xl | xxl
Section.behavior: vertical padding from spacing token, container max-width applied, horizontal padX responsive

Marquee.behavior: horizontal infinite scroll, ~30s loop, pause on hover, gap 64px
Marquee.use: logo wall, large headline marquee on home

FAQ.structure: single-column accordion, full-width row, plus icon rotates 45deg on open

Reveal.behavior: wraps any block, IntersectionObserver triggers once, fade + 30px translate-up, stagger children by 80ms

routes.title: russle — Web design, development, and branding

content.label: • Independent studio
content.h1: A studio for ambitious teams who care how things look, feel, and work.
content.sub: russle is a one-person practice designing, building, and branding modern websites — without the agency overhead.

content.eyebrow: Trusted by teams across the UK
content.logos: PLACEHOLDER — 8 client logos as SVG, currentColor

items.title: Brand identity
items.body: Logos, type systems, colour, voice. The foundation everything else builds on.

items.title: Web design
items.body: Bespoke layouts, design systems, prototypes. Figma in, production-ready out.

items.title: Web development
items.body: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind. Fast sites you can actually maintain.

items.title: Motion & interaction
items.body: Microinteractions, page transitions, scroll choreography — never gratuitous.

items.title: Launch support
items.body: DNS, analytics, transactional email, on-call for the first month.

items.title: Iteration
items.body: Most sites get better after launch. I stay involved.

content.items: PLACEHOLDER — 3 case studies (image, client, sector, year, link to /work/[slug])

content.h2: A small process for big outcomes.

rows.title: Discovery
rows.body: We start with a 60-min call. You explain the business, the audience, the constraints. I leave with a shape of the work.

rows.title: Direction
rows.body: Within a week you get a brand or design direction document — tokens, type, references, the why behind every move.

rows.title: Production
rows.body: Iterative builds with weekly reviews. You see the work in the browser, not just Figma.

rows.title: Launch & after
rows.body: Domain, analytics, email, the lot. Then a month of free iteration once you've used the live site with real users.

content.quote: PLACEHOLDER — strongest 1-2 sentence quote from a client.
content.author: Name, Role

items.q: How long does a project take?
items.a: A typical site is 4–8 weeks from kickoff to launch. Brand-only projects are 2–4 weeks. I take one project at a time, so the timeline is about how much you can review, not how busy I am.
Most websites land between £4k and £15k depending on scope. Branding is £2k–£8k. I quote a fixed price after the discovery call — no hourly games.
Yes — happy to white-label or work as part of a larger team. Get in touch with the agency name and what you need.

items.q: What stack do you build on?
items.a: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind for the front end. Resend for email, Vercel for hosting unless you need otherwise. Backend depends on the project — Postgres + Express, Supabase, or just static.

items.q: Will I be able to update the site myself?
items.a: Yes. Either via a CMS layer (Sanity, Payload) or via simple Markdown files in a repo, depending on your team. We choose during discovery.

items.q: What if I already have a brand?
items.a: Even better — I'll work inside your existing system. The brand-direction doc still gets written, just inheriting your tokens.

content.sub: Tell me about it. I respond to every enquiry within 24 hours.

sections.accentRule: primary CTA uses accent fill (only accent on this section)

content.label: • About
content.h1: I'm a designer-developer who'd rather do one project well than ten projects fast.

items.title: Slow craft.
items.body: I take one project at a time. You get my full attention, not a Slack channel staffed by juniors.

items.title: Direct contact.
items.body: No PMs, no proxies. You talk to the person doing the work, every day.

items.title: Real software.
items.body: I write production code. Your site doesn't get handed off to a build team — it gets built by the person who designed it.

content.right: PLACEHOLDER — first-person founder story, 200-400 words. Where you started, what you learned, why russle exists.

content.label: • Contact
content.sub: Got a project in mind, a question about the work, or just want to say hello?

left.successCopy: Thanks — message received. I reply within 24 hours.
left.footnote: For a longer brief, use the project intake →

content.sub: If you'd rather talk than type, grab a slot. No commitment, no sales pitch.
content.fallback: If the embed doesn't load, email hello@russle.co.uk.

routes.title: Start a project — russle

content.label: • Project intake
content.sub: Eight short steps. Takes about 5 minutes. The more you share, the faster I can come back with a real proposal.
content.stepsSource: Lifted from /Users/henry/Desktop/russle/src/app/page.tsx (Guest Digital intake form), restyled to russle tokens, branded as russle, submission goes to /api/intake (Resend)
content.successCopy: Thanks — your project brief is in. I'll be in touch within 24 hours with next steps.

routes.notes: This is the Guest Digital questionnaire ported into russle's design system. Same step structure and field IDs (so the schema is identical), restyled with russle tokens. Form lives at the narrow container width (900px) for legibility.

content.sub: Tell me about it. I respond within 24 hours.

/api/contact.behavior: Validate (zod), send via Resend to hello@russle.co.uk, return { ok: true }

/api/intake.behavior: Validate (zod, all required client_name + client_email), format into Markdown email, send via Resend to hello@russle.co.uk, return { ok: true }

GoogleAnalytics4.loader: next/script with strategy='afterInteractive', mounted in app/layout.tsx
GoogleAnalytics4.consent: default-allow with a small dismissable banner linking to /privacy (Phase 4 if needed)

seo.sitemap: /sitemap.xml — auto-generated from routes, work MDX files
seo.ogImageRoute: /api/og — dynamic OG image with russle wordmark + page title (Satoshi 500)

accessibility.focusVisible: 2px accent ring (#DF5613) with 2px offset on all interactive elements
accessibility.altText: every image requires alt; case study cover/gallery alts come from MDX frontmatter
accessibility.semanticHTML: header/main/footer landmarks, h1 once per page, button vs link discipline

russle-site/.package.json: deps: next@16, react, typescript, tailwindcss@4, framer-motion, lucide-react, resend, zod, geist, gray-matter, next-mdx-remote

src/app/.layout.tsx: fonts (Satoshi local + Geist Mono via geist), Navbar, Footer, GA4 script
src/app/.page.tsx: home route — composed of section components
src/app/.globals.css: @theme tokens, font-face declarations, base layer resets

russle-site/.public/: favicon, og.png placeholder, logo.svg, client logos placeholder

phase3Plan.1_scaffold: create-next-app with TypeScript + Tailwind 4 + App Router at russle-site/
phase3Plan.2_fonts: download Satoshi variable woff2 from Fontshare into public/fonts/, install geist
phase3Plan.3_tokens: write globals.css with @theme tokens from this spec
phase3Plan.4_layout_primitives: Container, Section, Button, Card, Tag, Reveal, Marquee, Navbar, Footer
phase3Plan.5_home: compose home from section components with placeholder content per spec
phase3Plan.7_intake: port src/app/page.tsx → src/app/start/page.tsx, restyle with russle tokens
phase3Plan.8_work: work index + dynamic [slug] route + 3 placeholder MDX files
phase3Plan.11_install_run: npm install + npm run build to verify
